mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer demand, as measured by monthly retail sales (RSAFS), signals robust economic activity that directly boosts corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If RSAFS trending above 500,000 does not produce positive 5-day returns in at least 60% of observations over a 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above the neutral level of 2.5%, monetary policy becomes restrictive, raising discount rates and reducing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If in periods when FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate is below 55%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: An environment with low unemployment (UNRATE < 4.0%) and accommodative monetary policy (FEDFUNDS < 2.0%) signals a Goldilocks scenario where the economy grows without overheating, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the joint condition UNRATE<4.0 and FEDFUNDS<2.0 does not yield a median 20-day return above zero or a hit rate >55% over any 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%), rising interest rates are perceived as a policy mistake that suppresses growth and earnings, resulting in negative forward returns. In low inflation, the same move signals confidence and is positive.
failure_condition: If during high inflation periods (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%) the correlation between weekly FEDFUNDS changes and subsequent 20-day returns is not significantly negative (|r| > 0.2), reject.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions (INDPRO > 100, indicating above-trend industrial output), rising retail sales (RSAFS) confirm sustained demand growth, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If in expansion periods (INDPRO > 100) the correlation between monthly RSAFS growth and subsequent 60-day returns is below 0.1 or the hit rate is not above 55%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals a weakening housing sector and broader economic softness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: If after HOUST falls below 1200, the average 20-day forward return is not negative or the hit rate is below 55% over a 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate synchronized expansion in both supply and demand, reinforcing each other and generating positive corporate earnings momentum, leading to higher forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If months with simultaneous increases in INDPRO and RSAFS do not produce positive 20-day returns with a hit rate >55% and median return >0, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals a softening labor market that reduces consumer income and spending, leading to weaker corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If monthly increases in UNRATE (month-over-month > 0.1pp) do not lead to a median 20-day forward return below zero over a 5-year window, reject.
